Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Supplemental Discovery Requests
Off Calendar May-16-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Maria Yancich, Esq. (925.225.6838; maria.yancich@statefarm.com) of Jeanette N. Little & Associates for Defendant.
No other appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NATALIE TONDELLI-WALSH's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Defendant's Supplemental Discovery Requests:

Defendant NATALIE TONDELLI-WALSH ("Defendant") seeks to compel Plaintiff MAGUIE INAYA ("Plaintiff") to respond to Defendant's Supplemental Interrogatory and Supplemental Request for Production of Documents ("Supplemental Discovery"). Plaintiff did not serve responses to the Supplemental Discovery and has not opposed the instant motion.

Supplemental discovery requests under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2030.070 and 2031.050 are somewhat unique. They are designed to elicit later acquired information and documents that are responsive to prior interrogatories and document demands. The code is silent as to whether a party must serve any response when they have no information or documents to supplement prior discovery responses. Regardless, Plaintiff failed to respond at all and her counsel failed to meet and confer.

Defendant's motion is GRANTED IN PART as follows:

1. Within ten (10) days of service of the notice of entry of this order, Plaintiff may serve verified responses to Defendant's Supplemental Interrogatory and Supplemental Request for Production of Documents.
2. Should Plaintiff fail to timely serve supplemental responses as set forth above, then pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010 (d) and 2023.030 (c), this court issues an evidentiary sanction precluding the introduction of evidence not provided in the prior discovery responses and which should have been provided in response to Defendant's supplemental discovery requests.
3. Sanctions are issued against Plaintiff and her counsel in the amount of $322.84, to be paid to Defendant and her counsel within ten (10) days of service of the notice of entry of this order.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling via email to sspaulding@bcsslaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Spaulding, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
